name: Mongolian Grill
recipeIngredient: 2 lbs Boneless lamb from the upper part of the leg
2 cups Slivered green onion (cut in half lengthwise and sliced diagonally into 1/2-inch sections)
2 tablespoons Flower pepper salt (see recipe at end)
recipeInstructions:
1. Ask the butcher to bone a leg of lamb and freeze the shank portion for later use.
2. Smash 5 large slices of fresh ginger with the handle of a cleaver, put them in half a cup of water for 2 hours, squeeze out the juice, and discard the ginger slices.
3. Peel and crush 4 large garlic cloves into a paste, adding a little rice wine or vinegar to moisten.
4. Soak millet overnight in cold water.
5. The next morning, add lamb bones to a large pot with enough cold water to cover them, bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for several hours, skimming off fat and removing the bones. Strain the stock and discard solids.
6. Rinse millet several times, return to the strained stock, and simmer slowly until grains break up and soup thickens; season with salt. Turn off heat and set aside.
7. Trim all fat and skin from beef and lamb, wrap well, and freeze for 2-3 hours or until firm but not frozen. Slice meat thinly against the grain into strips about 1/8 inch by 2x4 inches.
8. Arrange beef and lamb in separate layers on platters; cover and refrigerate until ready to use.
9. Place green onion and parsley in bowls, cover, and refrigerate until ready to serve.
10. Put each dip ingredient in a separate bowl, place bowls on a tray, and cover.
11. Prepare the sesame rolls as desired.
